en: Listen to me in silence, O coastlands; let the peoples renew their strength; let them approach, then let them speak; let us together draw near for judgment.
zh: 眾海島啊！你們要在我面前靜默， 願萬民重新得力。 讓他們近前來，然後說話， 讓我們聚在一起辯論吧！

en: Who stirred up one from the east whom victory meets at every step? He gives up nations before him, so that he tramples kings underfoot; he makes them like dust with his sword, like driven stubble with his bow.
zh: 誰從東方激動了一個人， 憑公義把他召到自己的腳前來？ 他把列國交在他面前， 他制伏眾王； 他使他們在他的刀下化作灰塵， 他使他們在他的弓下像碎稭一樣被吹散。

en: He pursues them and passes on safely, by paths his feet have not trod.
zh: 他追趕他們，安然走過他的腳未曾走過的路。

en: Who has performed and done this, calling the generations from the beginning? I, the LORD, the first, and with the last; I am he.
zh: 誰定這事並且作成？ 誰從起初預定萬代呢？ 就是我耶和華，我是首先的，與最後的人同在的，也是我。

en: The coastlands have seen and are afraid; the ends of the earth tremble; they have drawn near and come.
zh: 眾海島看見了，就都懼怕； 地極也都戰兢； 他們都近前來。

en: Everyone helps his neighbor and says to his brother, "Be strong!"
zh: 各人彼此幫助， 對自己的兄弟說：“要堅強！”

en: The craftsman strengthens the goldsmith, and he who smooths with the hammer him who strikes the anvil, saying of the soldering, "It is good"; and they strengthen it with nails so that it cannot be moved.
zh: 木匠堅固金匠， 用鎚打光金屬的，鼓勵打砧的； 論銲工說：“銲得好！” 又用釘子釘穩了。 使偶像不致動搖。

en: But you, Israel, my servant, Jacob, whom I have chosen, the offspring of Abraham, my friend;
zh: 但你以色列，我的僕人啊！ 雅各，我所揀選、 我朋友亞伯拉罕的後裔啊！

en: you whom I took from the ends of the earth, and called from its farthest corners, saying to you, "You are my servant, I have chosen you and not cast you off";
zh: 你是我從地極抓來的， 我從地的遠方召你來， 對你說：“你是我的僕人， 我揀選了你，並沒有棄絕你。”

en: fear not, for I am with you; be not dismayed, for I am your God; I will strengthen you, I will help you, I will uphold you with my righteous right hand.
zh: 不要懼怕，因為我與你同在； 不要四處張望，因為我是你的 神， 我必堅固你，我必幫助你； 我必用公義的右手扶持你。

en: Behold, all who are incensed against you shall be put to shame and confounded; those who strive against you shall be as nothing and shall perish.
zh: 看哪！向你發怒的都必蒙羞受辱； 與你相爭的人，都要如同無有，並要滅亡。

en: You shall seek those who contend with you, but you shall not find them; those who war against you shall be as nothing at all.
zh: 與你爭競的人，你要尋找他們，卻找不著； 與你交戰的人，都必如同無有，好像虛無。

en: For I, the LORD your God, hold your right hand; it is I who say to you, "Fear not, I am the one who helps you."
zh: 因為我，耶和華你們的 神，緊握著你的右手， 對你說：“不要懼怕，我必幫助你。”

en: Fear not, you worm Jacob, you men of Israel! I am the one who helps you, declares the LORD; your Redeemer is the Holy One of Israel.
zh: 蟲子雅各啊！以色列人哪！你不要懼怕。 我必幫助你；這是耶和華說的。你的救贖主，就是以色列的聖者。

en: Behold, I make of you a threshing sledge, new, sharp, and having teeth; you shall thresh the mountains and crush them, and you shall make the hills like chaff;
zh: 看哪！我要使你成為打糧的器具， 又新，又鋒利，又有很多利齒； 你要揚淨群山，把它們壓得粉碎， 又使岡陵變成糠秕。

en: you shall winnow them, and the wind shall carry them away, and the tempest shall scatter them. And you shall rejoice in the LORD; in the Holy One of Israel you shall glory.
zh: 你必簸揚它們，風要把它們吹去， 旋風也要把它們吹散； 但你要靠耶和華喜樂， 以以色列的聖者誇耀。

en: When the poor and needy seek water, and there is none, and their tongue is parched with thirst, I the LORD will answer them; I the God of Israel will not forsake them.
zh: 困苦和貧窮的人尋求水，卻沒有水； 他們的舌頭因口渴而乾燥； 我耶和華必應允他們， 我以色列的 神必不離棄他們。

en: I will open rivers on the bare heights, and fountains in the midst of the valleys. I will make the wilderness a pool of water, and the dry land springs of water.
zh: 我要在光禿的高處開闢江河， 在山谷之間開闢泉源； 我要使曠野變為水池， 使乾地變成水泉。

en: I will put in the wilderness the cedar, the acacia, the myrtle, and the olive. I will set in the desert the cypress, the plane and the pine together,
zh: 我要在曠野種上香柏樹、 皂莢樹、番石榴樹和野橄欖樹； 我要在沙漠中栽種松樹、 杉樹和黃楊樹，

en: that men may see and know, may consider and understand together, that the hand of the LORD has done this, the Holy One of Israel has created it.
zh: 好叫人看見了，就知道； 思想過，就都明白， 這是耶和華的手所作的， 是以色列的聖者所造的。

en: Set forth your case, says the LORD; bring your proofs, says the King of Jacob.
zh: 耶和華說：“呈上你們的案件吧！” 雅各的王說： “把你們有力的證據拿來吧！”

en: Let them bring them, and tell us what is to happen. Tell us the former things, what they are, that we may consider them, that we may know their outcome; or declare to us the things to come.
zh: 讓它們前來告訴我們將要發生的事。 以前的是甚麼事，讓它們告訴我們， 好叫我們用心思想，得知事情的結局。 或者向我們宣告那要來的事。

en: Tell us what is to come hereafter, that we may know that you are gods; do good, or do harm, that we may be dismayed and terrified.
zh: 把以後要來的事告訴我們， 好叫我們知道你們是神； 你們作這些事，或降福或降禍，以致我們彼此對望，一齊觀看。

en: Behold, you are nothing, and your work is less than nothing; an abomination is he who chooses you.
zh: 看哪！你們屬於無有， 你們的作為都屬於虛空， 那選擇你們的是可厭惡的。

en: I stirred up one from the north, and he has come, from the rising of the sun, and he shall call upon my name; he shall trample on rulers as on mortar, as the potter treads clay.
zh: 我從北方興起一人，他就來了； 他從日出之地而來，呼求我的名； 他必來踐踏掌權的，好像踐踏灰泥， 又像陶匠踹泥一樣。

en: Who declared it from the beginning, that we might know, and beforehand, that we might say, "He is right"? There was none who declared it, none who proclaimed, none who heard your words.
zh: 誰從起初講說這事，使我們知道呢？ 誰從先前說明，以致我們說“他沒有錯”呢？ 誰也沒有說明， 誰也沒有宣告， 誰也沒有聽見你們的話！

en: I was the first to say to Zion, "Behold, here they are!" and I give to Jerusalem a herald of good news.
zh: 我首先對錫安說：“看哪，看看這些！” 我要把一位報好信息的賜給耶路撒冷。

en: But when I look there is no one; among these there is no counselor who, when I ask, gives an answer.
zh: 我看的時候，卻沒有一個人； 他們中間也沒有謀士，我問他們的時候， 可以回答一句話。

en: Behold, they are all a delusion; their works are nothing; their metal images are empty wind.
zh: 看哪！他們都是無有， 他們所作的都是虛無； 他們所鑄的偶像都是風，都是虛空。
